deals.title: Wah Kwong orders up to four tankers at DSIC
deals.summary: Wah Kwong, a Hong Kong shipowner, has placed an order for up to four tankers at the Chinese shipyard DSIC. This deal indicates the ongoing recovery in the shipping sector, driven by increasing demand for energy transportation. Investors may benefit from the growth in tanker orders and the potential for rising freight rates.

deals.title: Hyundai Rotem signs Ho Chi Minh City metro contract
deals.summary: Hyundai Rotem has signed a major contract to supply driverless metro trainsets for Ho Chi Minh City's metro Line 2. This deal demonstrates the growing demand for modern rail infrastructure in emerging markets and may attract investors to similar projects in the region. Hyundai Rotem's expansion into Vietnam also highlights the increasing competitiveness of Asian rail manufacturers.

deals.title: Conduent sells Public Transit business to Modaxo
deals.summary: Conduent has sold its Public Transit business to Modaxo, a Canadian passenger transport technology group, for US$164m. This deal highlights the ongoing consolidation in the rail technology sector and may create opportunities for investors in similar businesses. The sale also allows Conduent to focus on its core operations.

deals.title: Alstom acquires Bombardier EU maintenance contracts
deals.summary: Alstom strengthens its European maintenance footprint by acquiring the remaining EU maintenance contracts from Bombardier Transportation, covering 12 depots across France and Germany.

deals.title: Nordic Aviation Capital raises €1.2B green bond
deals.summary: NAC closes its largest ever green bond issuance to fund the acquisition of next-generation narrow-body aircraft, targeting A320neo and 737 MAX deliveries through 2028.

deals.title: CMA CGM acquires Hamburg Sud feeder network
deals.summary: CMA CGM expands its Northern European short-sea presence with the acquisition of Hamburg Sud's feeder network, adding 23 vessels and 8 port concessions.

deals.title: Safran & MTU expand CFM LEAP MRO joint venture in Poland
deals.summary: Safran Aircraft Engines and MTU Aero Engines commit €210M capex to expand their joint MRO facility in Warsaw, doubling LEAP engine overhaul capacity to meet surging demand.
